statements.sql: INSERT INTO silver.NestedFlowTarget (ProductAlternateKey, EnglishProductName, LoadMode) SELECT CAST(ProductID AS NVARCHAR(25)), ProductName, 'full' FROM bronze.Product WHERE ProductName IS NOT NULL

MERGE INTO silver.NestedFlowTarget AS tgt USING (SELECT CAST(ProductID AS NVARCHAR(25)) AS ProductAlternateKey, ProductName FROM bronze.Product) AS src ON tgt.ProductAlternateKey = src.ProductAlternateKey WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET tgt.EnglishProductName = src.ProductName, tgt.LoadMode = 'delta' WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT (ProductAlternateKey, EnglishProductName, LoadMode) VALUES (src.ProductAlternateKey, src.ProductName, 'delta')

refactor.extracted_sql: SELECT CAST(p.ProductID AS NVARCHAR(25)) AS ProductAlternateKey, CAST(p.ProductName AS NVARCHAR(50)) AS EnglishProductName, CAST(CASE WHEN EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM bronze.Product WHERE ProductName IS NOT NULL) THEN 'full' ELSE 'delta' END AS NVARCHAR(20)) AS LoadMode FROM bronze.Product p WHERE p.ProductName IS NOT NULL
refactor.refactored_sql: WITH import AS ( SELECT p.ProductID, p.ProductName FROM bronze.Product p ), logical AS ( SELECT CAST(i.ProductID AS NVARCHAR(25)) AS ProductAlternateKey, CAST(i.ProductName AS NVARCHAR(50)) AS EnglishProductName, CAST(CASE WHEN EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM bronze.Product WHERE ProductName IS NOT NULL) THEN 'full' ELSE 'delta' END AS NVARCHAR(20)) AS LoadMode FROM import i ), final AS ( SELECT ProductAlternateKey, EnglishProductName, LoadMode FROM logical WHERE EnglishProductName IS NOT NULL ) SELECT * FROM final

source_tables.summary: Both extracted and refactored queries source from bronze.Product exclusively.

output_columns.summary: Both queries produce identical columns: ProductAlternateKey (NVARCHAR(25)), EnglishProductName (NVARCHAR(50)), LoadMode (NVARCHAR(20)).

joins.summary: Neither query contains explicit or implicit JOINs; both operate on a single source table.

filters.summary: Both queries filter WHERE ProductName IS NOT NULL (refactored applies this at the final CTE level, equivalent to extracted's main WHERE clause).

aggregation_grain.summary: Both queries operate at row granularity with no aggregation; output is row-by-row from the source table.

issues.message: Both queries contain a correlated EXISTS subquery in the CASE expression that evaluates the same condition for every row. This is semantically equivalent but inefficient; consider materializing the result outside the CASE if performance is critical.
